description.p: Boo is a local speech-to-text overlay that captures audio from your microphone and transcribes it on-device using whisper.cpp. Press Ctrl+Shift+Space (after approving the shortcut on first launch) or click the Record button, speak, and the transcript is copied to the clipboard and pasted into the focused app.
Everything runs locally, no audio leaves your machine.
Preview on Linux: recording, transcription and both portal permissions are verified, but Boo has not yet been run on a real GNOME or KDE desktop. Bug reports are welcome.
A teardown fix and internal test-coverage hardening.

ul.li: Closing the dictation overlay while a recording is in progress now stops it immediately, instead of leaving a background transcription worker running until the app quits.

description.p: Reliability fixes and shared cross-platform model handling.

ul.li: Fixes crashes and use-after-free hazards when quitting or closing dialogs mid-operation: in-flight downloads and desktop-portal requests are cancelled cleanly and worker threads are joined at exit.
Model download verification (SHA-256) and best-model auto-selection now share one implementation across macOS, Linux, and Windows, so the three behave identically.
Corrects the release version stamping so every platform's artifacts report the same version.

description.p: In-app model management, better transcription, and local crash capture.

ul.li: First run offers a model download dialog (curated list, progress bar, checksum-verified) on every platform, no more copy-a-curl-command.
Settings gains a model switcher: one dropdown of models on disk plus downloadable ones; picking a model swaps it in place and the choice persists.
Sharper transcripts: beam search on the final decode and a cleanup pass that removes repetition loops.
Partial model files from interrupted downloads are detected and offered for re-download instead of failing at load.
Diagnostic logging plus local crash capture; a crash report is surfaced on the next launch and never uploaded.

description.p: Adds a native ARM64 Windows build.

ul.li: Windows on ARM (Copilot+ PCs, Surface, and Windows VMs on Apple Silicon) now has its own zip, rather than only an Intel build that ran under emulation.

description.p: Reliability fixes from the first automated UI test pass.

ul.li: Recording silence no longer produces made-up filler text; the take ends with "no speech detected", in both batch and streaming modes.
Launching without a speech model now shows the install instructions instead of exiting silently.
The waveform returns to its idle colour after a recording that captured nothing.
The Flatpak moves from the end-of-life GNOME 47 runtime to GNOME 50.

description.p: The consistency release: Linux now looks and behaves like the macOS build, plus a broad reliability and security pass.

ul.li: The overlay adopts the macOS design language: a stack of transcript cards with per-card copy and dismiss, the round record button that becomes a square while recording, a waveform that colours itself for idle, recording and transcribing, and a status line that always shows the hotkey and counts the seconds while you talk.
Whisper no longer invents text out of silence: non-speech annotations such as [BLANK_AUDIO] are dropped, and low-confidence segments are filtered.
Starting a new recording while the previous one is still transcribing is now handled safely instead of racing.
The optional VAD model download is verified against a pinned checksum before use, and portal tokens now come from the system random source.
The waveform stops redrawing when idle, so an open window no longer costs battery.

description.p: The Windows release: Boo gains an experimental third platform. Nothing changes on Linux.

ul.li: Windows 10+ (x86_64): WASAPI audio capture, a native Win32 overlay and tray icon, the Ctrl+Shift+Space hotkey, and clipboard plus paste delivery. Compile- and test-verified in CI on real Windows runners; not yet verified by a human on real hardware, hence experimental.

description.p: The speed release: transcription moved to the GPU, a faster engine, and streaming while you talk.

ul.li: whisper.cpp upgraded to v1.9.1, and inference now runs on the GPU via Metal on Apple Silicon; it had been CPU-only.
NVIDIA Parakeet is now a supported engine and the preferred model: near large-v3 accuracy at many times the speed. Drop the ggml conversion in your models directory and Boo picks it up.

Streaming transcription: with a Silero VAD model, phrases are transcribed at natural pauses while you are still talking, with live text in the overlay; stopping only waits for the final phrase. The VAD model (under 1 MB) downloads automatically on first run on both platforms.
With several models installed, Boo now picks the most capable one it recognizes instead of always base.en.
Text lands in the target app faster after you stop dictating (macOS paste path).
Building from source works on macOS 15 again.

description.p: Correctness, security, and a project-wide quality pass.

ul.li: Boo now finds any whisper model, not only the one it recommends. Previously, dropping a different model where the docs said to put it was reported as "no model installed".
The RemoteDesktop restore token is written owner-only (0600); it was world-readable.
Fewer permission prompts: macOS asks for Accessibility only when it first needs it, and Linux no longer re-runs the shortcut approval dialog on every launch.

description.p: Fixes found by running Boo on a real GNOME desktop, and by testing it the way a new user would.

ul.li: Boo now tells you when your desktop has no global-shortcuts portal. GNOME only gained one in version 48, so on GNOME 46 (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS) the hotkey cannot work for any app; previously Boo failed silently. Auto-paste is unaffected, so Boo stays usable from the Record button.

Non-English dictation works. The language was hardcoded to English, which silently translated other languages instead of transcribing them; set BOO_LANG (a language code, or "auto") when using a multilingual model.
A single recording is now capped at 10 minutes and stops automatically, transcribing what it captured. Previously the buffer grew without limit and could freeze the app for minutes inside whisper.
The menu bar now shows when Boo is transcribing, rather than snapping straight back to idle while whisper is still working.
Fixed a memory leak, a data race, and a latent use-after-free in the audio backends.

description.p: First Linux preview, and the first build for Intel Macs.

ul.li: Linux ships as a Flatpak. Audio capture, transcription, the global hotkey and auto-paste are all verified; Boo has not yet been run on a real GNOME or KDE desktop.
macOS now builds for Intel as well as Apple Silicon.
On-device speech-to-text with whisper.cpp, audio never leaves the machine.
Global hotkey (Ctrl+Shift+Space) via the GlobalShortcuts portal.
Transcripts are copied to the clipboard and pasted into the focused app via the RemoteDesktop portal.
